notes.code_summary_synthesized_from_diff: Replaced the per-location straight `line.lineBetween(centerX, centerY, x, y)` (previously inside the locations loop) with a new `_renderPathLayer({ locations, state, homeX, homeY, padX, padY, usableW, usableH })` method called ONCE before the node loop. The method draws quadratic bezier trails from a hardcoded home anchor to every location node.
notes.spec_item_1_bezier: Quadratic bezier with one control point per segment. Approximated with 20 line segments via g.lineTo (Phaser's Graphics API has no native quadratic stroke). Control point at midpoint plus 12% of segment length along the perpendicular direction. Smooth at the 2-3 px stroke widths used; no visible facets.
notes.spec_item_2_color_by_biome: Stroke color/alpha keyed off the destination region's biome (homeBiome forced to DESERT since neighborhood sits in arizona_desert). Endpoint sampling uses the existing biomeForWorldRegion helper from the terrain layer (no duplicate WORLD_REGION_BIOME map). Palette: DESERT 0xA08250 alpha 0.6, GRASSLAND 0x6E5A32 alpha 0.65, MOUNTAIN 0x3C3228 alpha 0.75, WATER skipped, default 0x786446 alpha 0.6. Locked-region paths multiply alpha by 0.35 for visual de-emphasis.
notes.spec_item_3_path_width: DESERT/GRASSLAND/default = 2 px. MOUNTAIN = 3 px. Spec asked for 2-3 / 3-4 ranges; worker chose the lower bounds, which reads cleanly at 900x615 parchment scale.
notes.spec_item_4_layer_order: _pathContainer at depth -50 — sits above terrain (depth -100) and below nodes (default depth 0 / Phaser auto). Confirmed by visual order in create(): _renderTerrainLayer → _renderPathLayer → _renderLandmarkLayer → location node loop.
notes.spec_item_5_dashed_pending: NOT IMPLEMENTED THIS CYCLE — TODO comment at WorldMapScene.js:306-308 documents that data/discoveryUnlocks.js does not exist yet (will be authored by world-discovery-quests). When it lands, the path-renderer block needs a short follow-up to switch loc.pending paths to dashed strokes per spec item 5.
notes.spec_item_6_water_skip: if (biomeA === BIOME.WATER || biomeB === BIOME.WATER) continue; at line 342. TODO(ferry) comment documents the future ferry-boat mechanic that should re-enable these as dashed water-crossing routes.
notes.spec_item_7_deterministic_curve_directio: hash2(0x9a7b, seedA, seedB) where seedA/seedB are sum-of-charCodes of the two endpoint ids. Odd hash → +1, even hash → -1. Stable across reloads/HMR cycles per the requirement.
notes.existing_path_code_line_range_replaced: Original 4-line block at the top of the locations for-loop (centerX/centerY/line/lineBetween) — was lines 162-167 in the prior committed file. Replaced with a single call site at line 160 (this._renderPathLayer call) plus the new method at lines 286-440.
notes.hash_function_used: hash2(seed, a, b) from utils/terrainNoise.js. Seed constant is 0x9a7b (worker's choice; collision-free with terrain's 0x5eed and landmarks's 0x5eed^0xABCD1234). String→int via sum-of-charCodes is stable and length-independent.
notes.discoveryUnlocks_status: File does not exist as of commit 9fec243. Worker did not implement dashed pending paths; documented TODO in code so the world-discovery-quests follow-up can pick it up.
notes.visual_overlap_observation: Current map only has arizona_desert and mountain_range regions, so almost all paths are DESERT-styled. With three locations all clustered in the desert, path arcs may visually cluster near the home anchor. Will look more varied once non-Sonoran regions are added.
notes.git_diff_summary: 351 insertions / 10 deletions in a single file. Insertions are concentrated in two zones: the path-call site (lines 153-184) and the new method body (lines 286-440). Deletions are the prior straight-line block + a small homeX/homeY rearrangement so both the new path call and the existing home marker reuse the same constants.
notes.performance_estimate: Render budget unmeasured by worker (no performance.now bracket — recovery limitation, since orchestrator can't synthesize a measurement that wasn't taken). Estimated < 2 ms based on: 3 paths × 20 line segments per path × single batched Graphics object = ~60 lineTo calls. Trivial vs. terrain's 588 fillRects.
